# ADR-###: <the decision, as a statement>

<!-- ADRs are for BIG, COSTLY-TO-REVERSE decisions. The test is cost-to-reverse: if undoing
     it would take a week, it's an ADR. Otherwise it's a one-line entry in
     docs/engineering/decision-log.md.

     ⚠️ New ADR files start at ADR-015 — ADR-013 and ADR-014 are already cited in CLAUDE.md
     as real decisions whose files were never written.

     Title the ADR with the DECISION, not the topic. "Feature boundaries are enforced by a
     ratchet" beats "Feature boundaries". -->

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD · **Status:** proposed | accepted | superseded by ADR-###

---

## Context

<!-- The forces at play. Measure rather than assert where you can — a number makes this
     re-readable in a year. What is the situation that makes a decision necessary NOW? -->

## Decision

<!-- What we are doing, in the active voice, numbered if there are several parts.
     Be specific enough that someone can tell whether a given change complies. -->

## Alternatives considered

<!-- One row per option with the actual reason it lost. "Do nothing" is an alternative and
     deserves an honest entry — sometimes it should win. -->

| Option | Why not |
|---|---|
| | |

## Consequences

### Positive

### Negative

<!-- Be honest here. An ADR with no negative consequences is an advertisement, not a
     decision record. -->

### Neutral

<!-- Things that change but aren't clearly better or worse — including what this decision
     deliberately does NOT do. -->

## How to comply

<!-- What a person should actually do when they hit this rule. Include the escape hatch,
     and make using it feel slightly bad. -->
